question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests with unique, never-matching route paths (triggering the !req.route branch in toOpenApiPath()) so that req.path is used verbatim as the Prometheus label value, causing unbounded label cardinality growth in requestTotalCounter, durationHistogram, requestSizeHistogram, and responseSizeHistogram, exhausting process heap memory and crashing the mirror-node REST process, resulting in total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests with arbitrarily long, unique URL paths that bypass route matching (req.route is null), causing toOpenApiPath() to return the raw req.path as a Prometheus label, creating millions of unique time-series entries in the MeterProvider registry, exhausting heap memory and causing an OOM crash that shuts down all transaction confirmation endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests and then immediately abort the TCP connection before the HTTP response finish event fires, causing inFlightCounter.add(1) to execute but inFlightCounter.add(-1) inside res.on('finish') to never execute, permanently incrementing the in-flight counter and leaking associated per-request closure state (responseSize, startTime, patched res.write/res.end), eventually exhausting memory and crashing the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sustained stream of requests with unique HTTP method strings (e.g., FAKEMETHOD1, FAKEMETHOD2, ...) that are accepted by the HTTP parser, causing req.method to be used as a unique Prometheus label value in labels = {method, path, code}, creating unbounded cardinality in all per-route histograms and counters, exhausting memory and crashing the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that produce a wide variety of HTTP status codes (by triggering different error conditions) combined with unique path values, causing the three-dimensional label space {method, path, code} in requestTotalCounter and durationHistogram to grow without bound, exhausting heap memory and causing a process crash that prevents all transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a malformed req.url value (e.g., containing null bytes, invalid percent-encoding, or extremely long strings) that causes new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') to throw a TypeError, which is uncaught inside metricsMiddleware() and propagates as an unhandled exception, crashing the Node.js process and shutting down the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a URL containing special characters that cause new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') to produce a pathname that, after normalization, equals metricsPath, bypassing the authentication check in authenticate(req) and gaining unauthenticated access to the Prometheus metrics endpoint, leaking internal service topology and metric cardinality data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely large Content-Length header value (e.g., 9999999999999) that parseInt(req.headers['content-length'] ?? '0', 10) parses as a valid large integer, causing requestSizeHistogram.record() to record an astronomically large value that corrupts histogram bucket state in the OpenTelemetry SDK, causing subsequent metric collection to throw exceptions and crash the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a Content-Length header set to a negative number or non-numeric string, causing parseInt() to return NaN or a negative value, and requestSizeHistogram.record(NaN, labels) or requestSizeHistogram.record(-1, labels) to trigger an exception in the OpenTelemetry SDK histogram implementation, propagating an unhandled error that crashes the middleware and shuts down the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path that causes metricsHandler() to be called multiple times (e.g., through hot-module reload or repeated middleware registration), causing initMetrics() to create multiple MeterProvider and PrometheusExporter instances that are never garbage-collected, leaking memory until the process crashes and the network loses its transaction confirmation API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes the res.on('finish') callback to throw an uncaught exception (e.g., by causing toOpenApiPath() to return a value that triggers an error in requestTotalCounter.add()), which, because it is inside an EventEmitter callback, propagates as an uncaughtException and crashes the Node.js process, shutting down all transaction confirmation endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a very large response-body request (e.g., by triggering an endpoint that streams gigabytes of data) causing responseSize to accumulate to a value exceeding JavaScript's safe integer range (Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER), causing responseSizeHistogram.record() to record an imprecise or corrupted value that destabilizes the OpenTelemetry histogram state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that metricsMiddleware() overrides res.write and res.end on every single request (even non-metrics requests) to create a prototype pollution or function shadowing scenario where a crafted request causes the override assignment res.write = function(...) to interfere with another middleware's res.write override, breaking the response pipeline for all subsequent requests?
